(3 comments)

Hi Ben, unfortunately this patch still has some race conditions =[

This patch is now 9 month old with 19th patch set, of which, the last 10 have been about closing that race, I think it may be time to take a step back and look for what we are doing wrong.

I am not sure my reviews really help you much. Most of the time I end up commenting: "this is still racy", which we can agree, is quite a poor review. I would like to make better reviews and I think right now, the main obstacle to this is that I do not know the reasonning behind every patch you post. To be honest, the last 4 patch sets looked quite random to me.

Overall, I think leaving a comment after each patch set explaining what the change brings to the patch would really make a difference. It might seem unnecessarily time consuming, but I think it can really make a difference.

By the way, you still haven't addressed the following points, in the spirit of what I explain above I expect more than just a "Done" ;-):

commit.subject: LU-7988 hsm: mark the cdt as stopped when its thread exits
commit.message:
LU-7988 hsm: mark the cdt as stopped when its thread exits

Use kthread_stop() to stop and join the coordinator thread. Only after
that step can the coordinator state be set to CDT_STOPPED. As a
coordinator doesn't stop instantly, this closes a race if the
coordinator is being restarted at the same time, leaving one thread
shutting down and a new one starting up.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
